title: Student Financial Aid - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Student Financial Aid 13 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community Blindness Veteran/Military Personnel Issues CLARK COLLEGE VETERAN CENTER OF EXCELLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides general information about VA education benefits, advises on VA eligibility, and assists individuals with applying for VA education benefits. Serves as a liaison between the school and the Department of Veterans Affairs to ensure compliance with VA education regulations and continued receipt of benefits for eligible students. Tuition waivers are available. 1933 Fort Vancouver Way Vancouver, WA 98663 Eligibility: Veterans, dependents, reservists, National Guard, and Active Duty Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (360) 992-2073 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE TRIO STUDENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with applying for scholarships, transferring to another university, tutoring, and career and college major exploration. Program participants will have access to a textbook, laptop, and calculator lending library and can qualify for two free college courses per academic year. 1651 Lexington Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Currently enrolled students of Clatsop Community College and one or more of the following: -First-generation college student (neither parent graduated from a 4-year college) -Low-income according to federal guidelines -Have a documented disability which impacts ability to participate in the educational experience Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 338-2346 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIVISION OF FINANCIAL REGULATION CONSUMER FINANCIAL SERVICES INFORMATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Consumer advocates provide information on a variety of consumer financial services issues, including: -Managing finances: Tips for managing finances, getting out of debt, and learning about state-chartered banks and credit unions -Protecting finances: Tips for avoiding investment fraud, protecting one's identity, and getting help with collections or repossessions -Loans and debt help: Information about personal loans, building credit, and resources for preventing or managing a foreclosure -Securities and investments: Information about investing and help choosing a financial professional -Student loan servicing: Student loan ombudsman receives, reviews, and attempts to resolve complaints from student loan borrowers, provides borrowers information about rights and responsibilities, and directs them to resources 350 Winter Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (888) 877-4894 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FEDERAL STUDENT AID FREE APPLICATION FOR FEDERAL STUDENT AID (FAFSA) Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Federal Student Aid, an office of the US Department of Education, provides grants, loans, and work-study funds for college or career school. Manages the student financial assistance programs authorized under Title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965. 400 Maryland Avenue SW Washington, DC 20202 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following Basic Eligibility Criteria: -Demonstrate financial need (for most programs) -Be a US citizen or eligible non-citizen and have a valid Social Security number -Be registered with Selective Service (males ages 18-25) -Be enrolled or accepted for enrollment as a regular student in an eligible degree or certificate program -Maintain satisfactory academic progress in college or career school -Have a high school diploma or recognized equivalent (e.g., GED, home school, enrollment in an eligible career pathway program) Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-8pm and closed on federal holidays (877) 433-7827 1-877-4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FEDERAL STUDENT AID FOSTER YOUTH TUITION & FEE WAIVER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tuition and fee waiver available to eligible foster youth students for the equivalent of 4 years of undergraduate study 400 Maryland Avenue SW Washington, DC 20202 Eligibility: Former or current foster youth: -Must have been in ODHS or a federally recognized Oregon Tribe's care for at least 180 days after age 14 -Must not have left care before their 16th birthday Adopted former foster youth: -Must have spent at least 180 days in substitute care -Must have been adopted on or after January 1, 2012 Youth who experienced homelessness: -Must have experienced homelessness for at least 180 days between the ages of 14 and 24 -Must have received services from an ODHS-contracted organization or a host home while homeless -Must have been at least 16 years old when last receiving services Hours: Application deadline: October 1-June 30 of the award year, or the student’s last day of class for the academic year (whichever is earliest) Email (800) 433-3243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON OPPORTUNITY GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A state-funded, need-based grant program for college students. Oregon Opportunity Grants are available to qualified students for up to four years of full-time enrollment. Awards are prorated for halftime enrollment. Students who are enrolled in degree programs of divinity, religious theology are not eligible. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Oregon resident for at least 12 months prior to college attendance and a US citizen or eligible non citizen -For dependent students, parent or guardian must live in Oregon -Out-of-state students who are members of Native American tribes with traditional ties to Oregon may be considered Oregon residents. -Student loan must not be in default or owe refunds on federal student grant -Be enrolled at least half time at a participating Oregon-based post secondary institution -Undocumented students, including DACA students, may be eligible for an Oregon Opportunity Grant if they meet additional requirements -Undergraduate student with no prior baccalaureate degrees -Prioritizes award based on student aid index Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON PROMISE GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps cover tuition costs at any Oregon community college for recent high school graduates and GED test graduates 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Be a recent Oregon high school graduate or GED test graduate -Be an Oregon resident for at least 12 months prior to college attendance -For dependent students, parents must also live in Oregon -Have no more than 90 college credits completed or attempted -Students may be subject to eligibility requirements based on their Student Aid Index (SAI) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON TRIBAL STUDENT GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funding to offset the cost of attendance at colleges and universities. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Must be an enrolled member of one of the nine federally recognized tribes in Oregon -Must be enrolled or accepted for enrollment as a student at an Oregon college or university -Must be enrolled at least half-time -Priority for Undergraduate and Master students -Applicants who have already earned a bachelor's degree are not eligible for the grant if they are working towards an associate's degree, a second bachelor's degree, or an undergraduate certificate. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION CHAFEE EDUCATION AND TRAINING VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funding to help current or former foster care youth with post secondary education and training. Final Chafee Grant disbursement must be received the term before the youth turns age 26. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Currently be in child welfare foster care, or -Had been in child welfare foster care for at least 180 days (six months) after applicants 13th birthday and exited substitute care at age 14 or older -Be a former foster youth who was adopted or entered into a guardianship on or after 13th birthday, and adoption/guardianship finalized after 9/1/2015 -Must be 25 or younger to apply Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION DECEASED OR DISABLED PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICER GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A grant supporting dependents of public safety officers who were killed or disabled in the line of duty. Applicant must not have achieved a baccalaureate or higher degree from any post secondary institution. Awards are made on an annual basis and may be received for a maximum equivalent to four academic years (up to twelve quarters or eight semesters), or until a bachelor's degree is earned, whichever comes first. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Be a dependent (natural child, adopted child or stepchild) of a public safety officer in the state of Oregon who suffered death or permanent total disability suffered by a public safety officer while on or off duty -Be under 25 years of age at the time of submitting the application -Be an Oregon resident for at least 12 months -Not have achieved a baccalaureate or higher degree from any post secondary institution Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON BARBER AND HAIRDRESSER GRANT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a one-time grant to students attending a participating school in barbering, hair design, or cosmetology. Funding is based on availability and is not guaranteed. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Must be attending a licensed school of barbering, hair design, or cosmetology cosmetology that is a financial aid-eligible -Enroll full-time in a program which is at least nine months long or 900 clock hours -Must not be concurrently enrolled in high school and the beauty or barbering program -Must not be in default with any federal student loans Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON NATIONAL GUARD STATE TUITION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funding for Oregon National Guard Members enrolled in an post secondary institutions. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Current National Guard member -Not have achieved a bachelor's degree or higher -Be enrolled and in good standing -Complete military basic training -Not be in default on any federal Title IV loan or owe a refund on federal Title IV funds previously received unless the institution giving funds determines that the student has made satisfactory arrangements to repay and has regained federal eligibility Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON TEACHER SCHOLARS PROGRAM GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funding for students attending post secondary college who are culturally or linguistically diverse that are enrolled and pursuing their preliminary licensure for teaching, school counseling, school social work, and school psychology. Grant is limited, and based on available funding. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Be an Oregon resident for at least 12 months prior to college attendance. -Attend and be in good standing at an eligible Oregon four year-public and nonprofit college or university -Be enrolled and pursuing an approved preliminary licensure program in teaching, school counseling, school social work, and school psychology -Be within two years of completing a licensure program (or the equivalent number of remaining credits for part-time programs -Applicants are prioritized based on Student Aid Index (SAI) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE VETERAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with processing educational benefits, creating an academic plan, and connecting to community resources and services. 26000 SE Stark Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Veterans, active duty, Reserves, National Guard, and veteran dependents and/or survivors Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 491-7346 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals achieve independence and equality by providing information and referral, employment information, literature, scholarship programs, and a blindness-related adaptive equipment store. The Free White Cane Program provides rigid fiberglass canes. 200 E Wells Street Baltimore, MD 21230 Eligibility: -Primarily blind and visually impaired individuals, but sighted persons may also join -Free White Cane Program: Must be blind or visually impaired, must be requesting the cane for personal use, and it must have been more than 6 months since a previous white cane request Hours: Varies by chapter National office: Monday-Friday 5am-2pm Email (541) 653-9153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an advocacy program that helps students and their families set and achieve goals in four key areas: Mind, Body, Spirit, and Context. Services include wrap-around services, advocacy services, college visits, life skills workshops, job training, financial aid assistance, school tutoring, after-school homework help, and the development of cultural pride and relevant skills. Incentives like stipends, meals, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and financial assistance for youth participants. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Portland Public School District: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth -Successful Families 2020: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within David Douglas and Reynolds school districts. -SUN Youth Advocacy: 5th-10th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within Centennial, David Douglas, Gresham-Barlow, Parkrose, Portland Public, and Reynolds school districts. OR Region 3: 5th-10th grade any race/ethnicity enrolled in the following schools: Alice Ott Middle, Cherry Park Elementary, Gilbert Heights Elementary, Gilbert Park Elementary, Parkrose High, Parkrose Middle, Prescott Elementary, Sacramento Elementary, and Shaver Elementary Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm, some evening hours for events Email (503) 288-8177 329 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER COLLEGE AND CAREER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration, school search and exploration, resources for standardized test preparation, admissions and scholarship assistance, and educational workshops. Virtual service only. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Scholarships must be a current or former NAYA participants and pursuing a post-secondary degree -All other services:Grades 9-12 and who self-identify as Native American Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 325-4803 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST NATIVE CHAMBER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, networking opportunities, mentoring, and technical assistance for businesses in areas of financial management, legal support, marketing, and more. Also offers a scholarship fund for American Indian and Alaskan Native students entering college. 6500 S Macadam Avenue Suite 175 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Business owners, entrepreneurs, and students within the Native American Indian and Alaskan Native communities Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 894-4525 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES YOUTH EXPERIENCING HOMELESSNESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a path to have tuition and fees waived for higher institutions of learning. The waiver does not cover books, supplies, parking, personal incidentals, fines, housing, or meal plans. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following criteria: -Are currently or were homeless for 6 months or more while between the ages of 14-24 -Received services from an ODHS contracted provider or housing supports while experiencing homelessness -At least 16 years old when last receiving services -Currently under the age of 25 -Has or will complete a FAFSA/ORSAA for the academic year when enrollment is sought -Currently enrolled in courses totaling one or more credit hours at an institution of higher education as a graduate student Hours: Not applicable Email (541) 280-9228 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Independent Living Program (ILP) helps youth who are, or were, in foster care to become self-sufficient adults. It gives an opportunity to learn valuable skills necessary to make a successful transition from state and Tribal custody to living on their own in the community. Services include access to case management and funds for continuing education and assistance with housing. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or former foster children ages 16-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-6969 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY OSU COLLEGE OF BUSINESS CENTER FOR ADVANCING FINANCIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advice, resources, and information to navigate college and make financial decisions, including self-study modules and coursework. Topics include paying for college (billing, financial aid, scholarships, and other resources), identity theft prevention, creating a budget, post-college financial planning, and more. 2751 SW Jefferson Way Corvallis, OR 97331 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Some services available outside of usual hours by arrangement. Email (541) 737-2551 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND YOUTH PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers youth programs with interactive academic and real-world experiences that build leadership, life, and social skills, self-esteem, and cultural pride. Programs are designed to prepare students for success, encouraging post-secondary education and professional careers. Services include scholarship listings, job readiness training, employment support, and internship opportunities. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Ages 12-25. Focuses on serving the African American community and additional communities of color. Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE TRIO STUDENT SUPPORT SERVICES DIVISION OF FINANCIAL REGULATION CONSUMER FINANCIAL SERVICES INFORMATION HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON OPPORTUNITY GRANT MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE VETERAN SERVICES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT NORTHWEST NATIVE CHAMBER NORTHWEST NATIVE CHAMBER OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES YOUTH EXPERIENCING HOMELESSNESS PROGRAM OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY CENTER FOR ADVANCING FINANCIAL EDUCATION URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND YOUTH PROGRAMS
